exemplar.title: M25 #3: How can we reconcile the relentless drive to pursue knowledge with the finite resources we have available? Discuss with reference to the natural sciences and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #3: How can we reconcile the relentless drive to pursue knowledge with the finite resources we have available? Discuss with reference to the natural sciences and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Discussion is focused on the prescribed title and makes recognisable links to two areas of knowledge (natural sciences and the arts).
Concrete, well-chosen examples (CERN, DeepSeek AI, Frank Stellaâs Black Paintings, Conradâs Heart of Darkness) illustrate how different resources influence knowledge production.
Counter-arguments are acknowledged (e.g. cybersecurity risks, ethical concerns), demonstrating some awareness of alternative perspectives.
Key terms such as âreconciliationâ and ârelentlessâ are clearly defined in the introduction, establishing scope and focus.
weaknesses: Analysis remains largely descriptive; claims (e.g. social trust from open-source research, minimalism âprovingâ truths) are asserted rather than unpacked.
Engagement with core TOK concepts (justification, perspective, ways of knowing) is implicit and not explicitly developed.
Transitions between examples and policy discussion lack coherence and signposting, weakening overall flow.
Conclusion restates points without synthesising insights or discussing broader implications for knowledge frameworks.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessment places the essay in the 3â4 band: it remains connected to the title, offers identifiable arguments and examples, and shows some evaluation of viewpoints, but lacks the depth, coherence and explicit theoretical grounding required for the 5â6 band. Assigning 4 out of 10 reflects the balance of strengthsâfocused discussion, relevant examples, some counter-argumentsâand weaknessesâdescriptive treatment, limited TOK concept integration, structural and analytical shortcomings. This allocation also matches the externally awarded total of 4.

annotations.comment: The stray word âignoreâ appears to be an unresolved editing artifactâthis distracts from the argument and suggests insufficient proofreading.
The CERN crowdfunding example is well chosen to illustrate how pooling financial and intellectual resources can sustain large-scale scientific knowledge production.
Acknowledging the cybersecurity risks of DeepSeek strengthens the discussion by introducing a relevant counterargument to unfettered openness.
Precise funding figures (e.g., CHF 1,255,461,050) require inline citation or reference to source material to strengthen credibility and academic rigor.
Transition from the CERN example to the discussion of policy is abrupt; clearer signposting would improve coherence between these distinct arguments.
The student restates the prescribed title clearly at the outset, establishing a focused scope for the discussion on reconciling the pursuit of knowledge with finite resources.
The claim that open-source research cultivates higher social trust is asserted without empirical evidence or examination of instances where trust may erode.
The introduction effectively defines key terms such as âreconciliationâ and ârelentless,â providing a conceptual foundation for the subsequent analysis.
The introduction lacks explicit engagement with core TOK concepts (e.g., justification, perspective), missing an opportunity to situate the question within the Theory of Knowledge framework.
The DeepSeek open-source AI model example is effective in demonstrating how collaborative intellectual resources can accelerate scientific knowledge production.
Frank Stellaâs Black Paintings are a strong arts example, clearly illustrating how resource constraints can encourage innovative conceptual shifts in knowledge production.
The assertion that international funding automatically prevents knowledge monopolies is unsubstantiated; a deeper analysis of power dynamics and access barriers is needed.
The brief mention of ethical trade-offs in allocating resources to CERN research is not developed to show how these concerns impact the justification of knowledge pursuits.
The policy proposal to abandon research misaligned with societal needs is asserted without outlining practical criteria or concrete examples of how such policies would operate.
The discussion of minimalism stops short of showing how the absence of metaphor directly contributes to different ways of knowing or understanding in the arts.
The biographical context of Joseph Conrad compellingly illustrates how emotional resource limitations can shape narrative form and knowledge conveyed in literature.
The risks of open-source AI are noted but not integrated into an evaluation of how ethical responsibility should reshape resource allocation strategies.
Broad generalisations that minimalism âprovesâ certain truths need deeper analysis and linkage to how knowledge claims are justified in the arts.
The final sentence in this section ends mid-thought, indicating an incomplete transition that weakens the flow into the conclusion.
To strengthen theoretical grounding, the student should integrate TOK concepts explicitly and discuss their relevance to each example and argument.
Highlighting Marlowâs impressionistic narration in Heart of Darkness effectively shows how authors adapt methods under emotional constraints.
The suggestion to abandon creative pursuits for self-preservation lacks criteria for when epistemic gains outweigh personal harm, limiting its analytical depth.
The conclusion largely restates previous points without synthesising the arguments to draw out new insights or implications for knowledge frameworks.
There is insufficient reflection on the broader significance of balancing resource limits and knowledge pursuit, leaving the reader without clear take-home insights.
The absence of explicit TOK terminology (e.g., âways of knowing,â âknowledge frameworksâ) weakens the theoretical coherence expected at higher levels.

reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a

on the list for the

The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

is focused on
the title and is

offered and are

There is some

different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
